title: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan
display_title: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan
wikitext:
[]: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan... Post by Eternal Yamcha » Mon Mar 12, 2007 4:15 pm Recently I've been running into people who disliked Evangelion. Now, I don't care whether you like it or not, it's your opinion... But if you start openly saying why you don't like it, I'm going to respond. If you're downright attacking it, I'm going to come out swinging. I'm a VERY defensive person and protective of things I consider dear to my heart (there isn't much, but Evangelion IS one of those things.) Now I'm actually starting to receive flack for being an Evangelion fan. People insulting me, being dicks and flaming the hell out of the series. They're the first to use the whole "It's my opinion!" card when you criticize them for liking the things they do, but when it inconveniences them, they'll fight it and keep pushing the subject. Really, I'm just venting, but I want to know if anyone else has gone through this type of thing before? Being among other people who share the same issues as I have will definitely be nice and remind me that there really is a point to staying on the internet... Since I am so close to completely quit going on it since all I find is a waste of time, space, sperm and life. The only example I can actually share with people here would be a forum that I frequent (I'm a moderator there) and a few experiences from a couple conventions I've gone to. I don't know if I CAN link out to that other forum from here (should check to see if it's in the rules) but I'll wait until I get verification. Again, anyone else ever receive crap for being an Evangelion fan? Not just an anime fan in general...

[]: Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Mar 12, 2007 4:23 pm Oho, oh yes. I'm known on numerous forums as "that crazy guy who loves Evangelion and is therefore bonkers" (not word for word, of course) and they never argue with me because I usually debunk their accusations >.> I don't mind. As Katayoku said in some thread once, Evangelion and it's fans are generally disliked across the internet. Hey, you enjoy the show, why should anyone else judge you for that?

[]: Re: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan... Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 12, 2007 4:33 pm Eternal Yamcha wrote: all I find is a waste of time, space, sperm and life.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 12, 2007 4:34 pm The Eva subforum at AnimeNation has collectively received some nice, smelly crap in its time. I haven't had many problems personally due to the fact that I barely ever talk to anime fans (generally the only other people who know that NGE exists) who do not incorporate NGE into their diet.

[]: Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Mar 12, 2007 4:40 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Nothing worse than the usual push and shove in /a/ when an Eva thread happens.

[]: Post by Eternal Yamcha » Mon Mar 12, 2007 4:47 pm I'm known at many forums as the one big Evangelion fan (I almost always have an Asuka avatar and banner at other forums) so when an Evangelion thread comes up it's generally directed at me (such as THIS thread) so I'll check it out. I don't go out of my way to incorporate Evangelion into everything I say, hell, I rarely bring it up. And to cover myself now, I can understand why people don't like the series... That's fine. I don't care if you hate it or love it, it's your opinion. You can express your opinions on it and I'm cool with it to a point... If you're just going to insult it and attack it, I'm going to come out swinging. Even at conventions I've had people find out that I'm an Evangelion and proceed to actually FLAME the series infront of me.

[]: Re: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan... Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 12, 2007 4:54 pm Eternal Yamcha wrote: Again, anyone else ever receive crap for being an Evangelion fan? Not just an anime fan in general...

[]: Re: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan... Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 12, 2007 5:03 pm Ornette wrote: when they found out I was a big Evangelion fan, they immediately taken aback and would from that point on give me really worried looks.

[]: Re: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan... Post by Hunter21 » Mon Mar 12, 2007 5:21 pm Eternal Yamcha wrote: Again, anyone else ever receive crap for being an Evangelion fan? Not just an anime fan in general...

[]: Re: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan... Post by C4 » Mon Mar 12, 2007 6:10 pm Eternal Yamcha wrote: Really, I'm just venting, but I want to know if anyone else has gone through this type of thing before? Being among other people who share the same issues as I have will definitely be nice and remind me that there really is a point to staying on the internet... Since I am so close to completely quit going on it since all I find is a waste of time, space, sperm and life.

[]: Post by Kaminoyouni » Mon Mar 12, 2007 8:10 pm sadly i have two of these people as friends(i begin to question why more and more) i showed them the whole series(witch in retrospect i wonder wether that was a good idea or not) they contantly pester me with such mindless bable such as "gundum could beat eva blah blah blah blah". if your your going to bring up the 5th angel and the positron rifle when i say this, i would think that it had a lot more power behind it then one single gundum weapon ever had(have only seen most of gundum wing). but yes i know your pain i find it rare to find eva fan in my area of residence.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Mar 12, 2007 8:23 pm I don't get any flak for being an Evangelion fan. Any flak I get is usually from friends in jest. They tease me for being a head uber dork of the eva community, but they also know that what I do comes from the heart. I get much more flak from being into anime. Even among my social sphere, people often stereotype me as "the anime geek", which is strange to me because I don't even really watch anime anymore.

[]: Post by BobBQ » Mon Mar 12, 2007 9:12 pm Just remember the sage advice of your elders, and fuck 'em if they can't take a joke.

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 12, 2007 9:47 pm Since I run in different circles of various levels of geekiness, I get one of three responses on this. The response I get the most is from people who have never seen any anime: different levels of indifference. The other is pro-Eva with varying degrees on fanatacism. I have only met one person who absolutely hated Eva :evil: , though it wasn't that he didn't like other anime. He was a real Naruto/InuYasha nut and just loathed the way Anno ended Eva(both TV ending and EoE), interestingly enough.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 12, 2007 9:55 pm This happens in all mediums. If you're a fan of Nickleback or any nu-metal band (Korn, Limp Bizkit, etc.) you had better stay a good 500 yards away from a guitar forum or prepare to flame-broiled. I imagine NGE receives so much hate from part of the anime world because it's pretty much the Star Wars of the genre. Everyone who has the least bit of interest in anime, manga, or any art form related to it has heard of NGE. In fact, it's probably one of the first you hear about when you get into it. Its influence has been ginourmous. Over the entire anime spectrum. It's ended up with a huge and devoted following. And people are still discussing the series, in depth, some 10+ years after it first aired. To my knowledge, no other anime series can claim as many accolades as NGE has. To my knowldge, no anime series has had this much impact and lasting effect on the community. It's a series that, much like Star Wars or Star Trek, just will not die because the fans won't let it. Because it has effected them so much and perhaps even changed them. The opposite of this though is the people who just don't get it. They watch it, and just don't experience what many of us had. This is the difference in people. Instead of saying "well, I just didn't get it", they feel much more comfortable bashing something they don't understand. I'd fall out of my chair if someone gave an intelligent, negative critique of NGE. Hell, even someone that loves it as much as myself could point out some flaws in NGE, but I can't think of any work of art without a single flaw. To be flawless is to not be human or made by humans. And NGE's strengths, and great number of things it accomplishes outweigh the flaws by such a number that it just becomes a matter of whether or not the people viewing it pick up on those accomplishments or not. I will say though, to play devil's advocate a bit here: I can understand where many of the negative reviews come from. NGE is NOT an easy show. It's the same as people who viewed Mulholland Drive once and gave it a condemning review. Much like NGE, it's a very difficult work filled with so much depth, complexity, and subtleties that it's impossible to pick up on everything in one viewing. And those who want easy entertainment usually dismiss these types of shows for the very reason that they're not willing to put in the time and effort it takes to REALLY watch and understand them. Ok, end of my semi-rant too.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 12, 2007 10:12 pm BTW, if you've ever noticed in negative reviews, they often go something like this: "Series was great through the first 10 episodes or so." I never watch the first 10 episodes anymore (ep. 9 is the only exception). These were pretty much child's play in comparison the brilliance that came after. Anyone who likes the first half of the series but hates the rest is your first sign that: 1. They're probably a young, immature sci-fi fan that bought NGE expecting an exciting show featuring big robots. 2. After the first 13 episodes, when the show turned out to be about much more (and in reality, about something else besides) big robots fighting aliens, immature teenager loses interest because he doesn't actually want to try to follow along with the undercurrents that are happening throughout these episodes. "I can't stand the characters. Shinji is so whiny, depressed, and emo-kid. All the women are screwed up." Who here was super composed, level headed, and Mr./Ms. Cool when they were 14? Now, imagine that: 1. Your mom 'vanished' when you were a kid. 2. Your dad abandoned you. 3. Your dad called you back at age 14 to pilot a giant robot to save the world. I don't know about any of you, but I don't think I would be Mr. Cool Hero Dude here and be all. "I'll do it. Because the nation needs me. I'll do it for the children. For the sake of puppies and kittens everywhere. Yes, I'll do it, I'll fight, and I'll never give up! God Bless America!" To me, Shinji's reactions throughout the show ARE MUCH MORE FUCKING ACCURATE TO HOW A NORMAL 14 YEAR OLD WOULD REACT TO WHAT THE BLOODY HELL IS GOING ON AROUND HIM! I remember reading somehwere that Anno said something along the lines of that if you were perfectly normal and could relate to people perfectly fine then you wouldn't get anything out of NGE. I don't think that's necessarily true, because I'd say I'm pretty normal and relate to people well, but I also have a true love for art for and am able to sympathize with characters and works that are true to life. For people not to relate to NGE they're either too normal for their own good and incapable of understanding what it's like to be any of the characters in NGE (depressed, and feeling helpless most of the time), or they're just lying to themselves. Ok, now [/rant].

[]: Post by Kaminoyouni » Mon Mar 12, 2007 10:34 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: <snip>

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 12, 2007 11:07 pm Actually, I just became a fan a short while ago. Just saw the series for the first time a few months back. But this concept is universal within all artistic mediums. X makes a big splash within its genre, has a huge influence on it, has an effect on the people, creates a loyal fanbase, and ends up lasting for years because of it. The other side of the coin is the people who just don't get X, and instead of saying "I don't get it", they decide to bash what they don't understand, because it's easier. I see this in film, music, iterature, comics/graphic novels, etc. etc. etc. It's no different anywhere you look. There will always be those works that make a tremendous impacts, create a huge following, and the people who "don't get it" feel left out and decide to throw rocks at it and the people who like it.

[]: Post by Kaminoyouni » Mon Mar 12, 2007 11:16 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: and the people who "don't get it" feel left out and decide to throw rocks at it and the people who like it.

[]: Re: Catching flack for being an Evangelion fan... Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 12, 2007 11:44 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I think you have to be flawed in the right way before Eva can possess you; if you aren't, it washes by, or annoys.
